title: Telegram lifecycle commands — /exit + /clear (AFK removed per Ash)
depends_on: T-122 (bubble queue is the AFK buffer)
current_state: Telegram has no explicit lifecycle commands. To end a session cleanly, user must close the chat or wait for natural session timeout. There is no way to reset context without ending the whole bot. AFK / batching is impossible.
structural_problem: Sessions in Telegram are ambiguous — when does one end? When does context get cleared? Without explicit commands, the user has no agency over their session lifecycle.
target_state: Two commands: /exit (force-flush open bubble, run caretaker.lite, run session_exit steps, send goodbye), /clear (force-flush open bubble, dispatch under old session_id, then rotate session_id; memory preserved). AFK/back removed from scope per Ash decision.
design_notes: /exit — call BubbleCollector.flush(chat_id) first; emit synthetic 'exit' to process_input; reply 'Session ended. Send any message to start a new one.'
/clear — call BubbleCollector.flush(chat_id) first; dispatch the flushed bubble under current session_id; THEN rotate self.session_id; reply 'Context cleared. Memory preserved.'
Both commands bypass the bubble (they ARE the lifecycle event)
Both commands bypass the thinking layer (they are not thoughts)
success_criteria: /exit during open bubble: bubble's response sent BEFORE goodbye
/clear during open bubble: bubble's response sent under old session_id; new session has empty messages
risk_notes: Confusion risk: user forgets they /afk'd and wonders why Pi is silent. Mitigation: Pi acknowledges /afk with a clear status message; /afk auto-clears after 1h with notification.
migration_plan: 4. Wire /clear to session_id rotation + bubble dispatch under old session
